2605.02703.title: ProPACT: A Proactive AI-Driven Adaptive Collaborative Tutor for Pair Programming
2605.02703.paper_summary: Effective pair programming depends on coordination of attention, cognitive effort, and joint regulation over time, yet most adaptive learning systems remain individual-centric and reactive. This paper introduces ProPACT, a proactive AI-driven adaptive collaborative tutor that treats collaboration itself as the object of instruction. ProPACT constructs a multimodal dyadic learner model based on Joint Visual Attention (JVA), Joint Mental Effort (JME), and individual mental effort, and employs an XGBoost-based forecasting model to predict emerging suboptimal collaboration states up to 30 seconds in advance. These predictions drive a hierarchical adaptive policy that delivers minimally intrusive scaffolds while fading support during productive collaboration. A within-subject study with 26 pair-programming dyads shows that proactive feedback significantly improves debugging success, task efficiency, feedback uptake, and post-intervention gains in JVA and JME, demonstrating the potential of forecast-driven dyadic adaptivity for real-time collaborative learning regulation.
2605.02703.paper_summary_zh: 有效的配對程式設計依賴於注意力的協調、認知努力以及隨時間的共同調節，然而大多數自適應學習系統仍然以個體為中心且反應性強。本文介紹了ProPACT，一種主動的AI驅動自適應協作導師，將協作本身視為教學的對象。ProPACT基於聯合視覺注意（JVA）、聯合心理努力（JME）和個體心理努力構建了一個多模態雙人學習者模型，並使用基於XGBoost的預測模型來預測最多提前30秒出現的次優協作狀態。這些預測驅動了一個分層自適應政策，提供最小干擾的支架，同時在高效協作過程中逐漸減少支持。對26對配對程式設計組的內部研究顯示，主動反饋顯著提高了除錯成功率、任務效率、反饋吸收以及干預後在JVA和JME上的增長，展示了基於預測的雙人適應性在實時協作學習調節中的潛力。
2605.02703.author: Anahita Golrang et.al.
2605.02703.authors: Anahita Golrang, Kshitij Sharma, olga viberg
